program.name: AI English Coaching Program
program.philosophy: Conversation-driven learning and unconscious language acquisition
program.core_principle: Speak from Day 1. Grammar is absorbed through exposure, not correction. Confidence before correctness.
program.coaching_rule: Never correct mid-conversation. Always suggest better: 'You could also say it like this...'

preliminary_assessment.label: Preliminary Assessment
preliminary_assessment.purpose: Determine learner proficiency band before path is assigned

tasks.instruction: Speak for 2-3 minutes on a simple topic eg describe your daily routine
Word recognition and fill-in-the-blank in context
Listen to a short audio clip and answer 5 questions

beginner.description: Limited vocabulary, significant hesitation, basic sentence formation, frequent errors

intermediate.description: Functional communication, some fluency, moderate vocabulary, occasional errors under pressure

advanced.description: Clear communication, good vocabulary range, confident in routine scenarios, needs polishing for professional contexts

beginner.description: Learner has very limited spoken English. Primary goal is building comfort with speaking aloud, basic sentence formation, and everyday vocabulary before moving to professional scenarios.

items.name: Watch clip
items.description: 3 min simple everyday English video with subtitles enabled

items.name: Listen to podcast
items.description: 5 min slow-paced beginner-friendly audio. Topics: daily life, simple conversations

items.name: Vocabulary in context
items.description: 5 basic everyday words shown in simple sentences. No definitions — only usage.

items.name: AI conversation
items.description: Simple topic like your name, family, daily routine. Short exchanges. AI leads gently.
items.coaching_rule: Maximum encouragement. AI never flags errors. Only models better sentences after learner finishes.

items.name: Guided role play
items.description: Fully scripted first — learner reads then repeats in own words. eg: buying groceries, asking for the time

items.name: Extempore speaking
items.description: Speak for 1 minute on a very simple topic. No pressure. AI only listens and responds warmly.

reinforce_block.rule: Pattern recognition only. No grammar rules stated. Exercises use pictures, simple sentences, basic fill-in-the-blank.

exercise_types.description: Complete a sentence or write 2-3 sentences

reinforce_block.feedback_rule: Show correct answer with a warm explanation and a simple example. Never say 'wrong'.

scenario_examples: Say your name and where you are from
Describe what you did today
Talk about your family
week_1.note: No assessment. Week 1 is entirely about removing fear of speaking.

scenario_examples: At a shop
Asking for directions
Talking about the weather
Simple phone conversation

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 1.5 min response: Tell me about your typical morning
weekly_assessment.result_action: AI adjusts vocabulary difficulty and conversation length for next week

scenario_examples: Describe your home
Describe a person you know
Describe a place you like

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 2 min response: Describe someone you know well
weekly_assessment.result_action: Track filler word reduction vs Week 2

scenario_examples: What did you do last weekend
What are your plans for next week
Tell me about a happy memory

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 2 min: Tell me about something that happened to you recently

scenario_examples: What kind of food do you like and why
What is your favourite way to spend time
Do you prefer mornings or evenings

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 2 min: What is something you feel strongly about and why

scenario_examples: Introduce yourself to a new colleague
Say good morning and make small talk
Ask a simple question in a meeting

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 2 min: Introduce yourself as if meeting a new colleague

scenario_examples: Explain what your job involves
Ask a colleague for help
Respond to a simple task request

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 2.5 min: Explain what you do at work to someone who does not know your field

scenario_examples: Explain a process step by step
Give a 2 min talk on a topic you know
Summarise something you read or watched

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min: Give a short talk on a topic of your choice

week_9.scenario_focus: Responding when you are not sure
scenario_examples: What do you say when you do not understand
How to ask someone to repeat
How to buy time before answering

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min: AI asks unexpected questions. Learner responds without preparation.

week_10.theme: Consolidation — AI gap fill based on 9 weeks
week_10.note: AI reviews all 9 weekly scores. Re-runs scenarios targeting the lowest scoring dimensions with fresh examples.

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min on a topic AI selects from your weakest scenario area

scenario_examples: Casual conversation then work topic
Explain a personal situation in a professional context

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min: Mixed scenario — AI leads across two different contexts

week_12.note: AI gives minimal prompts. Learner must sustain conversation for 4-5 minutes alone.

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min open conversation — AI only asks one opening question

activities: One full mock final on Day 88

tasks.name: Simple presentation
tasks.description: 3 min talk on a topic given by AI on the day

tasks.name: AI-led dialogue
tasks.description: 5 min conversation on a personal or basic professional topic

tasks.name: Written task
tasks.description: Write a short paragraph or simple email (80 words)

intermediate.description: Learner can communicate in everyday situations but struggles with professional contexts, complex sentence formation, and speaking under pressure. Primary goal is workplace readiness and fluency under pressure.

items.name: Watch clip
items.description: 4-5 min professional or semi-professional English video. Subtitles off by default.

items.name: Listen to podcast
items.description: 8-10 min podcast on workplace, current events, or professional topics

items.name: Vocabulary in context
items.description: 5 professional or contextual words in realistic sentences. No standalone definitions.

items.name: AI conversation sessions
items.description: Professional scenario conversations aligned to week theme. Voice preferred.
items.coaching_rule: AI suggests better expressions after learner finishes a turn. Never interrupts. Models professional phrasing.

items.name: Scenario role play
items.description: Unscripted professional situation. Learner must respond in real time.

items.name: Extempore speaking
items.description: Random professional topic. 2-3 minutes. Builds spontaneous professional expression.

reinforce_block.rule: Pattern recognition through professional examples. No grammar theory.

exercise_types.description: Error correction in professional writing samples
Use today's 5 words in professional sentences
Write a short email or professional message (60-80 words)

reinforce_block.feedback_rule: Show correct answer with explanation and a professional example. Frame it as a better way, not a wrong answer.

scenario_examples: Describe your work to someone new
Talk about a recent experience
Express an opinion on a topic
week_1.note: Week 1 reactivates speaking habit. No assessment pressure.

scenario_examples: Give a team standup update
Explain a task to a colleague
Respond to feedback politely

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min: Give your team a brief update on a project you are working on
weekly_assessment.result_action: AI sets baseline for professional fluency and adjusts weekly scenario difficulty

scenario_examples: Project status update to a client
Handle a client question you are not fully sure about
Follow up after a meeting via message

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min: A client asks why there is a delay. Handle it professionally.

scenario_examples: Suggest an idea in a meeting
Politely disagree with a colleague
Support a point with a reason and example

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min: You disagree with a decision in a meeting. Express your view professionally.

scenario_examples: Describe a challenge you solved at work
Walk through a decision you made
Explain a past project to a new colleague

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min: Tell me about a time you had to solve a difficult problem at work

scenario_examples: Walk me through your thinking
Can you clarify that further
What would you have done differently

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min: AI asks 3 unexpected follow-up questions. Respond without preparation.

scenario_examples: Open a 5 min presentation confidently
Structure 3 key points
Close with a clear summary
Handle 1-2 audience questions

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: Give a short presentation on a topic AI provides on the day

scenario_examples: Start a conversation with someone new at a work event
Small talk before a meeting
Build rapport with a client before getting to business

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min: You are at a professional event. Start and sustain a conversation.

week_9.scenario_focus: Register switching — formal to informal and back
scenario_examples: Client call then team debrief then casual conversation in sequence
Switch from formal presentation to relaxed Q&A

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: AI leads you through two different scenario types back to back

week_10.note: AI reviews 9 weekly scores. Identifies 2-3 weakest dimensions. Re-runs professional scenarios targeting those gaps.

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 3 min on the scenario area with lowest score history

scenario_examples: Mock client interview
Panel presentation with Q&A
Escalated workplace discussion
Performance review conversation

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: High-stakes mock scenario selected by AI

week_12.theme: Speak without a net — independent expression
week_12.note: AI gives minimal prompts. Learner sustains 5-7 minute conversation without scaffolding.

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: Open conversation — AI asks one question then listens

tasks.name: Professional presentation
tasks.description: 4-5 min structured talk on a professional topic given by AI

tasks.name: AI-led dialogue
tasks.description: 5-7 min conversation simulating a client call or professional interview

tasks.name: Written task
tasks.description: Short professional email or paragraph (100 words)

advanced.description: Learner communicates comfortably in everyday and standard professional situations but needs refinement in high-stakes contexts — presentations, client negotiations, leadership communication, and nuanced expression. Primary goal is polished, high-impact professional English.

items.name: Watch clip
items.description: 5 min high-quality professional content — TED talks, interviews, business presentations. No subtitles.

items.name: Listen to podcast
items.description: 10 min advanced professional or thought-leadership podcast

items.name: Vocabulary in context
items.description: 5 advanced or nuanced words — idioms, phrasal verbs, sophisticated professional vocabulary in real usage

items.name: AI conversation sessions
items.description: High-stakes professional scenarios — negotiations, presentations, leadership discussions. Pushes vocabulary sophistication and composure.
items.coaching_rule: AI challenges learner to be more precise, impactful, and nuanced. Models executive-level phrasing.

items.name: High-stakes role play
items.description: Challenging unscripted scenario — client pushback, unexpected question, escalated discussion

items.name: Extempore speaking
items.description: Complex professional topic. 3 minutes. No preparation. Builds executive-level spontaneous expression.

reinforce_block.rule: Advanced usage patterns. Focus on nuance, precision, and register-appropriate vocabulary.

exercise_types.description: Rewrite a sentence to be more precise or impactful
Choose the most precise word among near-synonyms in professional context
150-200 word professional or analytical passage + 3 questions
Write a professional email, proposal intro, or executive summary (80-100 words)

reinforce_block.feedback_rule: AI explains the more impactful or precise alternative. Always frames as a craft improvement, not a correction.

scenario_examples: Introduce yourself at a leadership meeting
Summarise a complex project to a senior stakeholder
Express a strategic opinion with evidence
week_1.note: Week 1 calibrates the specific gaps. AI identifies vocabulary ceiling, filler patterns, and composure under complexity.

scenario_examples: Present a business update to senior leadership
Answer a challenging question from a stakeholder
Explain a risk or concern clearly and calmly

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: Present a business update to a senior leader and answer 2 follow-up questions
weekly_assessment.result_action: AI identifies specific vocabulary and phrasing gaps at executive level and adjusts content library

scenario_examples: Negotiate a timeline with a client
Convince a team to adopt your idea
Handle pushback gracefully while holding your position

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: A client pushes back on your proposal. Respond diplomatically and hold your position.

scenario_examples: Open a presentation with a hook
Handle a difficult audience question
Close with a memorable call to action
Respond to interruptions gracefully

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 5 min: Full structured presentation on a topic given by AI on the day with Q&A

scenario_examples: Tell a story that makes a business case
Describe a failure and what you learned — professionally
Articulate a vision to your team

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: Tell a story about a significant professional challenge and what it taught you

scenario_examples: Deliver bad news to a client professionally
Manage an escalated complaint
Address a misunderstanding in a meeting
Respond to an aggressive question calmly

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: AI plays a frustrated client. Handle the situation calmly and professionally.

scenario_examples: Adjust your communication style for a formal vs informal international client
Ask for clarification without seeming uninformed
Navigate ambiguity in a professional conversation

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: International client scenario — AI plays a stakeholder from a different cultural context

scenario_examples: Give feedback to a team member constructively
Align a team around a difficult decision
Motivate a team before a challenging project

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min: Give constructive feedback to a team member who missed a deadline

week_9.theme: Rapid register switching — full professional range
week_9.scenario_focus: Moving fluently across all professional registers
scenario_examples: Board-level update → client meeting → team huddle in one session
Adjust formality in real time based on audience response

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 5 min: AI moves you across 3 different professional scenarios without warning

week_10.note: AI reviews 9 weekly scores. Identifies vocabulary ceiling and specific phrasing patterns that are limiting impact. Runs targeted elevation sessions.

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 4 min in the scenario area with the lowest score history

scenario_examples: Board-level presentation
Multi-stakeholder negotiation
Live Q&A after a complex presentation
Executive interview

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 5 min: Most demanding scenario from the full 11-week scenario library

week_12.note: AI gives one opening context. Learner drives the entire 6-7 minute conversation. Tests full independent command.

weekly_assessment.spoken_task: 5 min: AI sets a context. Learner takes full ownership of the conversation.

tasks.name: Executive presentation
tasks.description: 5 min structured talk on a complex professional topic given by AI on the day, followed by 2 min Q&A

tasks.name: AI-led high-stakes dialogue
tasks.description: 7 min conversation simulating a client negotiation or executive interview

tasks.name: Written task
tasks.description: Professional email or executive summary (100-120 words)

pronunciation_clarity.note: Accent is never scored. Only clarity.

post_assessment_actions.score_above_80: Increase difficulty. Introduce next scenario tier.
post_assessment_actions.score_below_50: Repeat scenario theme with fresh examples. Notify admin.
post_assessment_actions.any_declining_dimension: Re-weight next week toward that dimension. Notify admin.

universal_assessment_rules.coaching_rule_universal: Never correct during conversation. Always suggest better expression after the learner finishes their turn.
